items.title: Dashboard: show full item JSON (CLI/agent parity)
items.body: Today the dashboard detail panel shows a short text summary (type, status, priority, body, blocked fields, predecessor refs). Agents use `axonflow item show --json` (and `--notes`) and receive the full ItemDto payload plus envelope: blockingPredecessors, blockedBy, optional recentNotes. Goal: the dashboard should surface the same structured fields so humans see exactly what is stored and what tooling sees—formatted JSON and/or structured sections, not only prose lines.

items.title: Document gap: snapshot card data vs item show --json envelope
items.body: Enumerate ItemJson.ItemDto fields (id, ref, type, stream, status, title, body, priority, parentId, blockedByWorkItemId, blockedReason, pathHints, snoozedUntil, discoveredFrom, clientKey, externalRef, assignedTo, sortOrder, completedAt, createdAt, updatedAt) and compare to item show --json top-level keys; map blockingPredecessors, blockedBy, recentNotes to dashboard data sources.

items.title: UX: detail view for full payload (formatted JSON, copy, optional Notes tab)
items.body: Specify formatted JSON (pretty-print, monospace), syntax-friendly styling, copy-to-clipboard; optional tab or section for recentNotes when parity with item show --notes matters.

items.title: Wire dashboard selection to display full ItemDto-equivalent object
items.body: On card select, render JSON object matching item show item (all scalar/nested fields including pathHints, clientKey, timestamps, discoveredFrom). Fold in predecessor/blocking context to mirror CLI envelope where data exists in snapshot or sibling queries.

items.title: Kestrel phase: GET item by ref returning item show --json-compatible body
items.body: Optional lazy detail: HTTP route returns same shape as item show --json (item + blockingPredecessors + blockedBy + notes optional) for strict parity and smaller initial snapshot.
